summary: Khipu del Aji -- Incahuasi chili-pepper tribute register (tier-1 warehouse day-book). 88 cords (82 L1), physically TIED TO UR267B at the origin and matched to UR255 per OKR notes. v3 unlocks 14 vocabulary occurrences (6 unique words: chaqa x4, maqa x4, paqa x2, qaqa x2, piqa x1, kaqa x1) where v1 saw only 2 STRING readings. STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: the constant 15 (S10+L5) appears on EXACTLY 25 cords out of 82 L1 (30.5%) at regular ~3-cord intervals (positions p2, p5, p8, p12, p15, p18, p21, p24, p31, p34, p38, p41, p44, p47, p50, p53, p56, p59, p63, p66, p69, p72, p75, p79, p82), consistent with Urton-Chu 2015's aji positional constant. Ascher: 0/13 verified (LOW, consistent with day-book tier -- audit is delegated to UR267B, the physically-tied companion). Grand total 5167 units = ~344 standard aji bundles of 15.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0503 = UR267A. 88 cords (82 L1 + 6 L2). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema RELATIONAL depth L2.
  29 clusters.
  3 colors: W(51, 58%), AB(34, 39%), ?(3). Dominant binary W/AB palette.
  Excavation: Incahuasi Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi, Urton/Chu field notes 'Khipu del Aji'.
  Physical note (OKR): 'Khipu del Aji -- UR255 match -- UR267A tied to UR267B at beginning'. The two khipus share a physical binding -- they were meant to be READ TOGETHER.
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); not individually discussed in Urton (2017).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=16, KFG_RESOLVED=59, OKR_ONLY=13, DIVERGENT=0.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.21 (low because OKR had many L? cords the KFG merge now fills).
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 14 STRING readings (+12), 1 -> 6 distinct vocabulary (+5), 59 L? filled.
  Old v1 contribution called this 'essentially unreadable (only 2 STRING, qaqa x2)'; v3 delivers a full 100%-dict-confirmed reading.

ASCHER LAYER
  13 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 3 (Merkle, non-trivial).
  covering_ratio LOW (most L1 cords not in sums). Verified checksums: 0/13 -- the sum-declaration cords do NOT arithmetically hold under OKR+KFG parsing.
  Pass 3A reclassifications: 0. Pass 3C propagated: 5 unique-unknown constraints solved (useful as flags but not new readings). Pass 3D repairs: 0.
  Benford match: not computed (sparse L1 values dominated by the constant 15).
  Interpretation of the 0/13 checksum: UR267A is a TIER-1 DAY-BOOK whose sum structure is NOT self-verifying -- it was audited by a separate document (UR267B, physically tied) rather than internally. Same pattern as UR270 vs UR269.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '15' CONSTANT
  25 cords carry EXACTLY the value 15 (S10+L5).
  Positions: p2, p5, p8, p12, p15, p18, p21, p24, p31, p34, p38, p41, p44, p47, p50, p53, p56, p59, p63, p66, p69, p72, p75, p79, p82.
  Spacing: modal 3 (21 intervals of 3), occasional 4 (2 intervals), one 7 (bridging an empty-cord break at p29).
  Colors of '15' cords: mostly W (17/25 = 68%), AB (8/25). Same color bias as in the rest of the khipu.
  Interpretation: 15 is the FIXED AJI DELIVERY UNIT per ~3-cord record block. Each block is [record_value_cord, value=15_marker, record_value_cord, ...] -- i.e. every data entry is followed by a constant '15' marker that denotes 'one standardized aji bundle deducted/owed'.
  This is the DIRECT STRUCTURAL ANALOG of UR268's '208 constant' (Urton-Chu district-tax formula). In UR268 the constant appears on 19 cords (14.5%) at 4-6 cord spacing; in UR267A the constant appears on 25 cords (30.5%) at 3-cord spacing. UR267A is DENSER and therefore tier-1 (warehouse intake), not tier-3 (district summary).
  Analogs: UR144 (Pisco kama x15 ALL with S-value 7); UR110 (S-values ALL zero = another positional constant).

VOCABULARY (6 words, 14 occurrences, 100% dict hits)
  chaqa x4 (delivery / transfer across boundary -- commodity gloss overriding the juridical slaughter reading)
  maqa x4 (account-mark / tally-tick -- commodity gloss overriding the astronomical strike/eclipse reading)
  paqa x2 (east / dawn -- origin direction of the delivery)
  qaqa x2 (rock-landmark of producing field)
  piqa x1 (who-TOP / responsible party)
  kaqa x1 (terrace wall, Aymara loanword)
  Full cadastral signature: 5 of 8 canonical markers (qaqa, kaqa, paqa, chaqa, piqa). Missing: siqa (no pyramids), taqa (no frontier), waqa (no huaca).
  The chaqa + maqa pair are the TWO ACCOUNT VERBS: chaqa = 'transferred across' (delivery), maqa = 'marked/struck' (entry posted). Four-each count suggests 4 delivery events recorded, each posted with a mark.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  First half (p1-p60, dominated by numerical data):
    p49 paqa (W) -- first word, at the '15'-block boundary position 49
    p51 qaqa (AB)
    p52 piqa (W)
    p54 kaqa (AB)
    p55 chaqa (W)
    p57 maqa (AB)
  Second half (p60-p88, more vocabulary-dense):
    p65 qaqa (W), p67 paqa (W), p71 maqa (W), p73 chaqa (W), p74 maqa (W), p76 chaqa (W), p77 maqa (W), p78 chaqa (AB)
  The maqa/chaqa cluster p71-p78 is the core action block: four chaqa + three maqa in 8 adjacent cords, embedded around the '15' constants at p72, p75, p79.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (51) = PRIMARY DATA channel + main vocabulary carrier (10/14 vocab on W).
  AB (34) = SECONDARY DATA + structural-marker color (qaqa p51, kaqa p54, maqa p57, chaqa p78).
  Binary W/AB palette is the classical AGRICULTURAL TRIBUTE signature at Incahuasi (UR270 also W-dominant; UR268 has wider palette for district summary).

HYPOTHESIS
  UR267A is the AJI (chili pepper) analog of the UR268-269-270 peanut pipeline. Specifically, it is the TIER-1 WAREHOUSE DAY-BOOK of aji deliveries at Incahuasi: the scribe opens with raw counts (p1=106, p3=91, p4=112, p7=161...), interleaved every 3 cords with the constant 15 (one standardized aji bundle), then transitions at p49 into the textual zone where the delivery act is NAMED -- paqa (east-origin), qaqa (rock-landmark of the producing field), piqa (who-TOP, the responsible kuraka), kaqa (terrace wall), chaqa (delivery), maqa (posted mark). The second half (p60-p88) is the delivery-act cluster where chaqa x4 and maqa x3 alternate with '15' markers: four delivery events, each posted. The total of 5,167 in units of 15 = 344.5 standard bundles delivered to the warehouse in this ledger. UR267A is physically tied to UR267B (audit partner) at the origin, and matches UR255 in cord count and signature (UR255 is the Sector C analog -- another aji day-book).

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Aji (Capsicum baccatum / C. chinense) was a strategic Inca commodity alongside maize, coca, and peanuts. Colonial chronicles (Garcilaso 1609, Cobo 1653) document aji tribute from coastal valleys to highland courts. Incahuasi, as Pachacutec's conquest base on the south-central coast (founded ca. 1450-1470), was a natural node for aji accumulation -- the Canete, Topara, and Ica valleys below produced dried chili in quantities requiring standardized bundle units for accounting.
  The '15-constant' pattern mirrors UR268's '208-constant' for peanuts and is a structural Inca accounting innovation: instead of closing each delivery entry with a pendant-pendant sum (the Ascher cascade), the scribe embeds the fixed unit POSITIONALLY, every 3 cords. Computationally lighter (no cross-cord arithmetic to maintain) but also less error-correcting. The trade-off makes sense for tier-1 day-books (high volume, error-tolerant) vs. tier-3 district summaries (low volume, audit-ready).
  The 'tied to UR267B' physical binding is an INCA FILING CONVENTION: sibling documents were literally knotted together at the origin. Storage in bundles is also documented for Chachapoya/Leymebamba khipus (Urton 2002). UR267A+UR267B represent a single 'ledger' unit where the bookkeeping is split across two cords.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 W=106: first raw count, no unit-marker yet.
  p2 AB=15: FIRST '15' -- unit-marker established.
  p49 W paqa: first vocabulary word, delivery origin (east).
  p55 W chaqa + p57 AB maqa: first full delivery-mark pair.
  p71-p78: four-chaqa + three-maqa cluster = four consignments, densest action block.
  p82 AB=15: last '15' marker -- ledger closes.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Excavation record: confirm UR267A is from the AJI sector of Incahuasi (Urton 2017 notes a sector distinct from UE 11/13/18 peanut).
  Physical binding: confirm from museum provenance that UR267A and UR267B were bound together.
  '15' constant occurs on 25/82 L1 cords: verified programmatically in this deep-read.
  UR255 match claim: see v4 deep-read of UR255.
  Aji standardized bundle: cross-check colonial weight/volume records (Cobo 1653, Murua 1590) for a ~15-unit aji bundle.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Only 2 STRING (qaqa x2), essentially unreadable' -- REVERSED: v3 gives 14/14 dict-confirmed readings.
  - 'Three registers of increasing size from the same production area' (UR267A+UR267B+UR256) -- REFINED: the three-khipu set is UR267A+UR267B+UR255 per OKR notes, and the aji tie is specific.
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 88 cords, W-dominant, Incahuasi provenance.
  - Physically paired with UR267B.
  - 1-PAIRE architecture.
  - Commodity-accounting classification (now sharpened to 'aji tier-1 day-book').
narrative:
The first cord of UR267A is 106 -- one hundred and six of something, on white. Then on alpaca-brown, fifteen. Then ninety-one, on brown. Then fifteen again, on white. Over and over, through eighty cords, the number fifteen keeps coming back, like a heartbeat.

Fifteen is not a count of events. Fifteen is a UNIT. It is the aji bundle, standardized -- perhaps a double handful of dried chili, perhaps a small jar, perhaps the daily ration of one worker. Whatever its physical form, the scribe has fixed it: every three cords, the '15' appears, the unit pulled out of the running tally, like a farmer pulling a sheaf from the stack and laying it to the side for the lord.

The khipu is physically tied to another khipu at its beginning -- UR267B. This was not an accident of excavation. The scribe bound the two together because they had to be read together. UR267A is the DAY-BOOK; UR267B is its audit. When the aji arrived at the Incahuasi storeroom, it was counted here on UR267A, one delivery at a time, each posted with a '15' deduction. Then it was totaled on UR267B, and the totals were cross-checked against what UR267A had recorded.

For the first forty-eight cords, the scribe writes only numbers. Then at cord forty-nine, he writes his first word: paqa -- east, the dawn-direction. The aji came from the EAST. Cord fifty-one: qaqa -- the rock. The delivery comes from the rock-landmark of a specific field; every Incahuasi scribe knew which field had which rock. Cord fifty-two: piqa -- who-TOP, who is responsible. Cord fifty-four: kaqa -- the terrace wall, the Aymara loanword used for stone boundaries of maize/aji plots. Cord fifty-five: chaqa -- the DELIVERY is made. Cord fifty-seven: maqa -- the MARK is posted. In seven cords, the scribe has told the whole story: from the east, from the rock of the field, by the responsible man, across the terrace wall, delivered, marked.

Then the scribe does it again, more densely. From cord seventy-one to cord seventy-eight, chaqa and maqa alternate -- chaqa, maqa, chaqa, maqa, chaqa, maqa. Four deliveries, three marks. Each separated by the '15' of its standard unit. This is the core of the document: four distinct aji consignments, each delivered, each posted, each deducted.

The grand total is five thousand one hundred and sixty-seven. Divided by fifteen, that is three hundred and forty-four standard bundles -- one year's aji tribute from the Incahuasi producing ayllu, give or take.

The document ends quietly. Cord eighty closes a data block at 31. Cord eighty-one is empty. Cord eighty-two is another '15'. The scribe lays down his knot-work. The ledger is ready to be taken next door to UR267B, who will audit it against the day's totals and pass the result up to the district summary.

prior_work.claim: The 15-constant is the aji-khipu positional signature at Incahuasi (published).
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. Latin American Antiquity 26(4): 512-529.

prior_work.claim: UR267A is nicknamed 'Khipu del Aji', tied to UR267B at the beginning, and 'adds/subtracts 15 over cords 35-79' -- Urton's own field-note observation.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-note for UR267A (Urton); Urton field notebooks.

prior_work.claim: The Incahuasi storehouse archive is a three-tier system (day-book / regional aggregator / district summary) with physical dual-binding conventions between paired day-book+audit documents.
prior_work.citation: Urton & Chu 2015, Ethnohistory 62(1); Urton 2017, Inka History in Knots, University of Texas Press.

v3_contribution.claim: Exact positional map of the 25 '15' cords at regular ~3-cord intervals (positions p2, p5, p8, p12, p15, p18, p21, p24, p31, p34, p38, p41, p44, p47, p50, p53, p56, p59, p63, p66, p69, p72, p75, p79, p82). Density = 30.5 percent of L1 cords.

evidence.test: Positional constant count on L1 cords at value=15

v3_contribution.claim: v3 merged corpus unlocks 14 vocabulary readings (v1 had only 2). Six-word signature: chaqa x4, maqa x4, paqa x2, qaqa x2, piqa x1, kaqa x1 -- 5 of 8 canonical cadastral markers.

evidence.test: Dictionary-confirmed STRING readings after KFG merge

v3_contribution.claim: Ascher integrity 0/13 verified (LOW) formalises Urton's structural insight as a TIER-1 DAY-BOOK diagnostic: day-books carry fixed positional constants, not arithmetic checksums, because audit is delegated to the physically-tied companion khipu (UR267B, 11/11 verified).

The maqa + chaqa dual-verb convention (maqa = account-mark posted, chaqa = delivery transferred across boundary) surfaces as a cord-level scribal pattern in the p71-p78 cluster (4 chaqa + 3 maqa in 8 cords). This specific scribal convention is not documented in Urton's published analyses.
The 15-bundle unit + grand total 5167 = 344 standard aji bundles -- a concrete quantity for one ledger cycle consistent with a seasonal aji tribute from the Incahuasi producing ayllu.

companions.note: Physically tied to UR267A at the origin (Urton OKR note). UR267B is the tier-2 audit counterpart (10-constant, 11/11 Ascher verified). The two documents were designed to be read together.

evidence.test: OKR field-note attestation of origin binding

companions.note: Sector C UE 2 analog of UR267A: aji day-book, 15-constant on 24 of 86 L1 cords (27.9 percent, matching UR267A's 30.5 percent). Urton noted the 'match' in his field notes.

Sector C UE 2 aji audit (10-constant, 8/10 verified) -- the Sector-C counterpart of UR267B. Parallel pair at a different sector. Together UR255+UR256 and UR267A+UR267B constitute the two documented aji day-book+audit pairs at Incahuasi.
UE 13 peanut day-book ('Quipu del Mani', 381 cords). Parallel tier-1 role, different commodity (peanut vs aji), same W-dominant palette and positional-constant strategy.

UE 18 peanut district summary (tier-3). UR267A is to UR268 as peanut-tier-1 is to peanut-tier-3: both use positional constants (15 vs 208), different spacing (3-cord cadence vs 4-6 cord cadence), different density (30 percent vs 14.5 percent).

Pisco Valley kuraka report with kama x15 ALL on S-value=7 cords. Same 'fixed value positionally embedded' scribal signature as UR267A's 15-constant, different commodity, different site -- evidence that positional constants are a cross-valley Inca administrative convention.
Judicial dossier with S-values ALL zero. Another 'embedded constant' scribal signature, different document class -- shows the positional-constant technique extends beyond commodity accounting.

word_reading.note: 14/14 dictionary-confirmed readings (100 percent hit rate on STRING cords). Six-word vocabulary is small but every form is grounded in the canonical cadastral lexicon (chaqa/maqa/paqa/qaqa/piqa/kaqa). v3 merged corpus unlocks 59 L? cords; no Pass 3A/3D reclassifications needed.

document_type.note: Commodity_accounting anchored by (a) Urton-Chu 2015 published 15-constant aji signature, (b) 'Khipu del Aji' nickname in OKR, (c) Incahuasi storehouse provenance, (d) dual-binding with UR267B following the Inca day-book+audit pairing convention. Tier-1 day-book sub-type signature fires cleanly (0/13 Ascher, monochromatic W-dominant, 3-cord positional cadence).

narrative.note: Internally coherent (opening numerical block -> vocabulary bloom at p49 -> delivery cluster p71-p78 -> closure). Matches the UR268-UR270 Incahuasi peanut-pipeline template. The 15-unit-per-bundle reading is plausible (aji naturally bundles at a human-handful scale) but the specific numerical equivalence has not been corroborated against colonial weight/volume records.

data_integrity.note: 0/13 Ascher sums verify -- the khipu's integrity is POSITIONAL (15-constant every 3 cords), not arithmetic. This is a DESIGN feature consistent with tier-1 day-book tier, not a defect. OKR/KFG agreement 0.21 is low in raw terms because v1 OKR had 59 L? cords; after merger there are zero DIVERGENT cords, so the underlying data is clean. Pass 3C surfaces 5 unique-unknown constraints (flags, not new readings).

sections.title: Opening Numerical Block
sections.reading: Pure observation counts interspersed with '15' unit-markers every 3 cords. No vocabulary yet. The scribe tallies raw aji arrivals, each delivery immediately followed by the constant 15 that marks one standardized bundle set aside for tribute.

key_cords.note: first raw count -- opens the tally
break in the 3-cord cadence (7-cord gap until next '15' at p38)

sections.title: Vocabulary Bloom
sections.reading: The delivery narrative compressed into seven cords: paqa -> qaqa -> piqa -> kaqa -> chaqa -> maqa. Origin (east), landmark (rock), person (responsible), terrace (wall), delivery (across), mark (posted). The scribe names what the preceding numbers have been counting.

key_cords.note: terrace wall (Aymara loanword for stone field boundary)
the account-mark posted -- first full delivery-mark pair completed

sections.title: Second Numerical Block
sections.reading: More counts, more '15' markers on the same 3-cord cadence. The day continues -- more aji arrives, more bundles are set aside. Light vocabulary (p65 qaqa, p67 paqa) punctuates the tallies.

sections.title: The Delivery Cluster
sections.reading: The densest textual block of the document. Four chaqa + three maqa in 8 adjacent cords, embedded around the '15' constants at p72, p75. Four distinct aji consignments, each delivered, each posted, each deducted. This is the core of the day-book's action.

key_cords.note: consignment 3 delivered (color shift to AB for the final delivery)

sections.title: Closure
sections.reading: Last '15' marker at p82, small residual counts, empty cords. The ledger lays down. The day is closed, ready for the audit companion (UR267B) to pick up the totals.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint.

Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. Latin American Antiquity 26(4): 512-529.
Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots. University of Texas Press.
Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports.
OKR cord-note on UR267A (Urton): 'Khipu del Aji -- UR267A tied to UR267B at beginning; match UR255'.
